# Mindcraft - Ecosystem Simulator

> An interactive 2D ecosystem simulator where carnivores, herbivores, and plants compete for survival. Each species is driven by a user-programmable brain built with Mindcraft -- a tile-based visual programming language.

Mindcraft programs are written by arranging tiles into rules. Each rule has a WHEN condition and a DO action. A collection of rules forms a brain that drives an autonomous actor. Three species -- carnivores, herbivores, and plants -- each run their own brain, which you can edit live in the browser.

## Pages

- [Ecosystem Simulator](https://sim.mindcraft-lang.org/): Live simulation with editable brains for carnivores, herbivores, and plants
- [Language Documentation](https://sim.mindcraft-lang.org/docs): Full tile reference, sensors, actuators, and programming guide

## Language Features

- Tile-based visual programming: compose behavior from typed, drag-and-drop tiles
- WHEN/DO rules: declarative condition-action rules evaluated every simulation tick
- Sensors: See (vision cone), Bump (contact detection), Random, Timeout, Current Page, and more
- Actuators: Move, Turn, Eat, Shoot, Say, Switch Page
- Multi-page brains: state machines using numbered pages
- TypeScript compilation: write brains in TypeScript, compile to Mindcraft bytecode via the VS Code Web extension

## Ecosystem Mechanics

- Physics-based movement using Matter.js rigid body simulation
- Three species: carnivores (hunt herbivores), herbivores (graze on plants), plants (stationary)
- Energy system: actors lose energy over time and regain it by eating
- Vision cones with line-of-sight occlusion
- Collision (bump) detection between actors
- Configurable population counts per species

## Technology Stack

- React 19 -- UI (sidebar, brain editor overlay)
- Phaser 3 with Matter.js -- game canvas and physics
- Vite -- build tooling
- Tailwind CSS v4 -- styling
- @mindcraft-lang/core -- Mindcraft language runtime (tiles, compiler, VM)
- @mindcraft-lang/ui -- shared brain editor React components

## Links

- Main product site: https://mindcraft-lang.org
- Language documentation: https://sim.mindcraft-lang.org/docs
- Open source on GitHub: https://github.com/humanapp/mindcraft-lang
